hero.title: BetterAlaminosCity.org
hero.description: Find permits, offices, ordinances, and city services — plus everything about the Hundred Islands National Park.

hundredIslands.heading: Discover Hundred Islands National Park
hundredIslands.body: Alaminos City is home to the Hundred Islands National Park, a protected marine reserve of over 120 limestone islands in the Lingayen Gulf — the Philippines' first national park and one of Pangasinan's most visited destinations.

history.heading: A Brief History of Alaminos City

entries.text: A Northern Zambal tribe founded the settlement of Suyang (in what is now Zaragoza, Bolinao).
Suyang's residents moved to Barrio Casborran (now Barangay Baleyadaan, Alaminos City); the community grew quickly and drew more settlers from eastern Pangasinan.
Casborran broke away from its mother town, Bolinao, and became an independent pueblo.
A dispute between the original settlers and newer migrants over politics and the economy ended in a fire that destroyed the town; survivors slowly returned to rebuild.
Rebuilding was cut short when renewed conflict forced residents to abandon the town for Nansangaan (now Barangay Inerangan).
Residents founded a new settlement on higher ground away from the coast and named it Sarapsap — today's Barangay Poblacion.
Sarapsap's growth caught the attention of Spanish Governor-General Juan Alaminos y Vivar during an official visit.
In recognition of his popularity, the town was renamed "Alaminos" in his honor.
Alaminos and eight other towns of northern Zambales were folded into Pangasinan province; Francisco Reinoso became the town's first Municipal President.
Alaminos rebuilt and its economy recovered after World War II.
The municipality was reclassified from fourth class to third class.
A further reclassification to first-class municipality followed a 338.5% rise in gross income.
President Gloria Macapagal Arroyo signed Republic Act 9025, converting Alaminos into a component city.
Voters ratified the change in a plebiscite, with 85% voting "Yes," making Alaminos Pangasinan's fourth city.

funFacts.title: Rebuilt From Disaster
funFacts.description: The town was burned down in 1758 amid a dispute between settlers, then displaced again in 1764 before finally taking root as Sarapsap — today's Poblacion — in 1766.

funFacts.title: Named Before He Was Governor-General
funFacts.description: Alaminos was named in 1872 to honor Juan Alaminos y Vivar, a year before he actually became Governor-General of the Philippines (1873–74).

funFacts.title: A Landslide Vote for Cityhood
funFacts.description: When Alaminos became a city in 2001, 85% of voters said "Yes" in the ratifying plebiscite, sealing its status as Pangasinan's fourth city.

funFacts.title: Home to the Philippines' First National Park
funFacts.description: The Hundred Islands, off the coast of Barangay Lucap, were declared the country's first national park by President Manuel L. Quezon in 1940.

history.sourceLine: Sources: City Government of Alaminos; Philippine government records.

contact.heading: Emergency Hotlines & Contact
contact.cityHall: City Hall: Quezon Avenue, Poblacion, Alaminos City, Pangasinan · Mon–Fri, 8:00 AM–5:00 PM

disclaimer.text: BetterAlaminosCity.org is a volunteer-run civic project. It is not an official website of the Alaminos City government.

footer.aboutBlurb: BetterAlaminosCity.org is a community-run guide to Alaminos City government services, home of the Hundred Islands National Park.

pageHeader.title: City Services Directory
pageHeader.subtitle: Find permits, applications, and assistance programs offered by Alaminos City.

pageHeader.title: Government Directory
pageHeader.subtitle: Meet the officials and offices serving Alaminos City.

about.missionBody: BetterAlaminosCity.org is a volunteer-run civic project. It is not an official website of the Alaminos City government — it aggregates publicly available information about city services, offices, and ordinances to make them easier to find. Content is drawn from official government sources where available and clearly marked as a placeholder where it isn't yet verified.
about.contributeBody: This project is open source and welcomes contributions from developers, translators, and residents who want to help verify content. Read the contributing guide and code of conduct before opening a pull request.
about.techStackBody: Built with React Router, the Kapwa civic design system, and i18next for English/Filipino translation, this site is fully open source under the MIT license and maintained by volunteers — not the Alaminos City government.
